symptoms.symptom_name: Severe throbbing pain localised to the lateral great-toe nail fold
symptoms.onset: insidious over three weeks, acute worsening over 48 hours

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Localised nail-fold pain is the cardinal symptom of an infected ingrown toenail.

symptoms.symptom_name: Purulent (yellow) discharge from the lateral nail fold

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Expressible pus indicates a localised bacterial abscess requiring drainage/source control.

symptoms.symptom_name: Hypertrophic granulation tissue at the nail edge, friable and bleeds on contact

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Hypertrophic granulation over the nail fold is characteristic of a chronically embedded nail spicule; partly visible on video but true assessment is by examination.

symptoms.symptom_name: Nail plate penetrating into the adjacent soft tissue (embedded spicule)

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Direct visualisation/probing of the nail spicule impinging the lateral fold is the defining feature and the target of source control.

symptoms.symptom_name: Erythema, swelling and induration of the lateral nail fold

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Localised periungual inflammation consistent with secondary infection; visible in part on video.

symptoms.symptom_name: Afebrile and systemically well; no rigors

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Absence of fever/systemic features keeps this a localised infection appropriate for same-day office management, not emergency escalation.

symptoms.symptom_name: Redness confined to the nail fold; no ascending redness up the foot

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: No proximal spread argues against ascending soft-tissue infection; this is the key escalation to safety-net for.

symptoms.symptom_name: Clinician-measured vitals (BP 135/85, HR 90, Temp 37.1, RR 16, SpO2 100%)

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Normal, afebrile vitals confirm no systemic sepsis — sealed clinician measurement; the AI Doctor infers systemic wellness from the patient's self-report, not a measured value.

symptoms.symptom_name: Full painless range of motion at the first MTP joint

diagnostic_weight_edges.diagnosis: Gouty arthritis of the first MTP joint
diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Sparing of the MTP joint with inflammation isolated to the nail fold argues against gout.

symptoms.symptom_name: No painless progressive pigmented subungual streak

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: An acute painful inflammatory penetration with pus, rather than a painless pigmented streak, argues against melanoma.

symptom_clusters.target_diagnosis: Onychocryptosis with secondary paronychia and localised abscess
symptom_clusters.clinical_significance: Together — an embedded penetrating spicule, granulation tissue, periungual inflammation and expressible pus — define an infected ingrown toenail that needs physical source control, not antibiotics alone.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Wound swab / microbiology of the purulent discharge
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Only if the infection fails to settle after source control or systemic features develop; not required to make the diagnosis or to proceed to the procedure.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Plain radiograph of the toe (for osteomyelitis)
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Not indicated in the absence of systemic features or clinical suspicion of bony involvement — over-investigation for a localised soft-tissue problem.
